rulings.Case Title: SANTIAGO ROBLETO VS. JULIA VARGAS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Wednesday, November 9, 2011, line 12, DEFENDANT JULIA VARGAS DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT Sustained without leave to amend on the issue of standing. Santiago Robleto Jr. may pursue his claims in his individual capacity, but lacks standing to bring the causes of action in the First Amended Complaint on behalf of the Estate of Dido Caldera. See Probate Code § 9820. Demurrer is overruled on all other grounds. Plaintiff has sufficiently stated causes of action for fraud, breach of fiduciary duty, and declaratory relief, and imposition of a constructive trust. Filing an amended complaint while a demurrer is pending is not a judicial admission that the initial complaint was insufficient. Plaintiffs' allegations in the First Amended Complaint relate back to the original complaint because they arise out of the same set of facts. It is not clear from the face of First Amended Complaint or in matters that can be judicially noticed that Plaintiffs claims are time-barred. Defendant's Request for Judicial Notice of Exhibit G (2007 letter) is denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/MM

rulings.Case Title: BRENDA OCHOA et al VS. ERLINDA SAN JUAN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion Andmotion For Judgemnt Creditors For A Finding Of Contempt As To Defts For The Failure To Comply With Two Court Orders
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, November 9, 2011, Line 1, PLAINTIFF BRENDA OCHOA, EVE SEGASTURE, JENNER OCHOA, MICHAEL CIOFALO Notice Of Motion And motion For Judgment Creditors For A Finding Of Contempt As To Defendants and their counsel For The Failure To Comply With Two Court Orders. Hearing required. Defendants' counsel are to bring a copy of the Notice of Stay of Proceedings, which was not attached as Exhibit A to the Notice of Filing of Notice Stay of Proceedings. Upon proof of the bankruptcy stay, the hearing will be taken off calendar pending the bankruptcy proceedings. The court is disinclined to separately hear the motion for contempt against Defendants' counsel. = (302/MLM)

rulings.Case Title: COIT VENTURE PARTNERS LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED VS. 240 LOMBARD CONDOMINIUM HOMEOWNERS ASSOCIATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Wednesday, November 9, 2011, line 2, PLAINTIFF COIT VENTURE PARTNERS LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION is denied. Plaintiff fails to sustain its summary adjudication burden under CCP 437c. The First Amended and Restated Enabling Declaration appears to be one of the controlling documents and Plaintiff does not substantively address that document. Request for Judicial Notice of First Amended and Restated Enabling Declaration is granted. =302/MM

rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Omtion For Order Directing Defts To Post Bond, Security Or Other Undertaking Or In The Alternative For Other Equitable Relief
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, November 9, 2011, line 7. PLAINTIFF 21ST CENTURY INSURANCE, FARMERS INSURANCE EXCHANGE, MID-CENTURY INSURANCE COMPANY Motion For Order Directing Defendants To Post Bond, Security Or Other Undertaking Or In The Alternative For Other Equitable Relief. rulings.Case Title: NIKOLAS LEMOS VS. PROGRESSIVE CHOICE INSURANCE COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, November 9, 2011, line 11. DEFENDANT PROGRESSIVE CHOICE INSURANCE COMPANY DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. The demurrer is overruled and the motion to strike is denied. The breach of contract cause of action is properly pleaded according to its legal intendment and effect. The bad faith cause of action is properly pleaded under Hightower v. Farmers Ins. Exchange, 38 Cal. App. 4th 853 (1995). The complaint includes sufficient facts to support a prayer for exemplary damages.
